query: UEs bounce between two neighbouring cells every few seconds - which mobility parameters stop the ping-pong?
notes: Failure mode named; direct terminology lane.

query: Two sites with heavy overlap keep swapping the same users, both mid-call and while their phones sit idle.
notes: Overlap flapping spans connected (TS-03) and idle reselection (TS-14); both should surface.

query: Stationary users near a cell border complain of overnight battery drain, and paging traffic has roughly doubled.
notes: Symptom-side only: battery plus paging load points at idle reselection churn.

query: On the high-mobility layer, measurement reports arrive after the serving signal has already collapsed - retune A3 offset and TTT?
notes: Late-report mechanism named with its parameters.

query: Calls from cars on the expressway die right where one sector ends and the next begins; coverage plots look clean.
notes: Effect-only, no RAN vocabulary; pure semantic test for too-late handover.

query: Seconds after moving to a new cell the phone loses it again and crawls back to where it came from.
notes: Bounce-back after HO paraphrased; no parameter names.

query: How should A3 offset and time-to-trigger be balanced so handovers fire neither too early nor too late?
notes: Tuning trade-off: too-late, too-early and the rebalance scenario all apply.

query: T304 keeps expiring during handover on one cluster - what do I check first?
notes: Timer named; checks exact-token anchoring.

query: Mid-handover the UE sends preambles but never hears a random-access response from the target, and the procedure stalls.
notes: No RAR = target RACH failure (TS-05); the same failure typically ends in T304 expiry (TS-04).

query: Walk me through how N310, T310 and N311 interact when a UE declares radio link failure.
notes: Counter and timer interplay named explicitly.

query: Users say the call freezes for a moment and then dies completely whenever they take the basement stairwell.
notes: Freeze = out-of-sync then T310 (TS-06); total drop = no cell for re-establishment (TS-07).

query: After a link failure the handset searches, finds nothing suitable, and the call simply ends - which timer bounds that search?
notes: T311 search window described without naming T311.

query: On the 26 GHz layer, sessions stall whenever the user turns around or a truck passes, then recover after a few seconds.
notes: FR2 blockage given as 26 GHz plus physical events; beam failure never named.

query: A drive test shows a strong cell on another carrier, but handsets behave as if it does not exist and later fail.
notes: Neighbour invisible on another carrier; measurement gaps never mentioned.

query: When do I need to configure measurement gaps so a UE can measure inter-frequency neighbours?
notes: Direct configuration question; exact-term lane.

query: We raised the A3 offset to suppress junk measurement reports and now every handover in the district triggers late.
notes: Cause-and-effect retold with the parameter named.

query: Xn handover requests keep getting rejected by the target gNB - we suspect admission control; how do we confirm?
notes: Interface and suspected cause both named.

query: Handovers between the two vendors' base stations fail before the phone is even told to move.
notes: Preparation-phase failure implied by 'before the phone is told to move'; Xn never named.

query: Right after a handover completes, downloads hang for several seconds even though the call itself stays up.
notes: User-plane stall after HO; path switch and NGAP never named.

query: CHO is set up on the fast corridor, yet no UE ever executes it and they still drop late.
notes: CHO named; execution-condition scenario.

query: In EN-DC the 5G leg keeps dropping while the 4G anchor call stays up - what does the UE report and what should I check?
notes: SCG described as the 5G leg; asks for the failure report by behaviour.

query: Edge users show full bars for downloads yet cannot complete access or handover - we suspect the reverse direction.
notes: Good DL but failed access: uplink-limited edge, stated without the word uplink.
